sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The AccuracyMetric class, implemented in Cython, is a specialized metric within the AnalysisG framework designed for comprehensive evaluation of model accuracy, particularly in the context of event-level predictions. It extends the MetricTemplate class, providing tailored functionalities for handling prediction scores, truth labels, and generating Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) curves. This class is crucial for assessing the performance of machine learning models in high-energy physics analyses.

listitem.para: cdef void merge_data(vector[plt_roc_t*]* oux, vector[plt_roc_t*]* inx) except+ nogil: An external C++ function (declared in <tools/merge_cast.h>) used for merging vectors of plt_roc_t* objects. The except+ nogil indicates that this function can release the GIL and can raise C++ exceptions that are converted to Python exceptions.

sect1.title: Helper Functions (C-style)
sect1.para: These functions are implemented as C-style functions in Cython for performance optimization.

listitem.para:
cdef tuple mx_index(vector[double]* sc): Finds the index and value of the maximum element within a C++ std::vector<double>.
sc
A pointer to a C++ std::vector<double> representing scores.
A Python tuple (index, max_score).

cdef void get_data(AccuracyMetric vl, dict data, dict meta): A callback function used by MetricTemplate.InterpretROOT to extract and store accuracy-related data from event data.
It identifies the processing mode (training, validation, evaluation) from the data dictionary.
Extracts ntops (number of true tops) and score (prediction scores).
Stores ntops_truth, edge_scores, and ntop_score into the AccuracyMetric object's internal C++ structures, organized by mode, epoch, model, and k-fold.
vl
An instance of AccuracyMetric.
data
A Python dictionary containing event data.
meta
A Python dictionary containing metadata (e.g., epoch, kfold, model_name).

cdef vector[plt_roc_t*] make_roc(plt_roc_t* out, modelx_t* inx): Prepares data for ROC curve generation. It iterates through the ntops_truth and ntop_score data within a modelx_t structure, creating plt_roc_t objects that encapsulate the scores and truth labels for each model and k-fold.
out
A pointer to a plt_roc_t object (used as a base for model and variable names).
inx
A pointer to a modelx_t object containing collected model data.
A C++ std::vector of plt_roc_t* objects, each representing a set of scores and truth for a specific ROC curve.

sect1.title: The AccuracyMetric Class
sect1.para: AccuracyMetric is a cdef class that inherits from MetricTemplate. It specializes in collecting and processing data relevant to classification accuracy, particularly for generating ROC curves.

listitem.para:
__cinit__(self): The Cython "constructor" equivalent.
Initializes self.root_leaves to specify which branches from ROOT files should be read for accuracy metrics (e.g., "ntop_truth", "ntop_scores", "edge" for training, validation, and evaluation modes).
Initializes self.root_fx to map these branches to the get_data helper function for processing.
Initializes a new C++ accuracy_metric object and assigns its pointer to self.mtx. It then upcasts self.mtx to an accuracy_metric* and assigns it to self.mtr for specialized access.

Postprocessing(self): This method is called after all data has been interpreted from ROOT files. It orchestrates the generation and plotting of ROC curves.
Data Aggregation: Iterates through self.event_level (a C++ map containing collected event data), aggregating data by epoch and model.
ROC Data Preparation: For each epoch and model, it calls make_roc to create plt_roc_t objects containing scores and truth labels.
Parallel ROC Building: Uses cython.parallel.prange to parallelize the process of building ROC data in the C++ backend. For each epoch, it creates an ROC object (from AnalysisG.core.plotting) and uses its build_ROC method to populate it with scores and truth.
Plotting: Finally, it calls rxc.__compile__() on each ROC object to generate and save the ROC curve plots.

sect1.title: C++ Interaction
sect1.para:
The AccuracyMetric class leverages Cython's capabilities for seamless C++ integration:
cdef class AccuracyMetric: Declares AccuracyMetric as a Cython extension type.
cdef accuracy_metric *mtr: A C++ pointer to an accuracy_metric object, which is the specialized C++ backend for accuracy calculations.
cdef plt_roc_t*, cdef modelx_t*: Pointers to C++ structs used for organizing ROC plotting data and model-specific data.
from AnalysisG.core.tools cimport *: Imports C++ declarations from tools.pxd, including has_string.
from AnalysisG.core.metric_template cimport MetricTemplate: Imports the base MetricTemplate class, from which AccuracyMetric inherits.
from cython.parallel import prange: Enables parallel execution of loops, leveraging multiple CPU cores for faster processing.
ROC rxc: An instance of the ROC class from AnalysisG.core.plotting, used for generating ROC curve plots.
